title: Chrysanthemum newesskyanum
intro: Chrysanthemum newesskyanum, also known as Korean chamomile or Neweisky's chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Korea and Japan. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum newesskyanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chrysanthemum newesskyanum is commonly prepared as a tea or used as a dried flower for making infusions, as well as being used in various tinctures and extracts.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum newesskyanum, also known as Newessky's chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Central Asia, specifically in the mountainous regions of Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan. As a member of the Chrysanthemum genus, it is closely related to other species of flowering chrysanthemums. The plant is often classified within the subgenus Chrysanthemum, which includes many species of chrysanthemums that are native to Asia. Chrysanthemum newesskyanum is often used in traditional medicine and is prized for its vibrant yellow flowers.
